html:
[1008]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 颇烦躁若厌人语 - Describes a state of restlessness or irritability, as if tired of hearing others speak.
  • 载吾城外有山水处置之 - Command to take someone to a place outside the city with mountains and water for rest or treatment.
  • 三日一来省吾 - Directive to visit every three days to check on the person's condition.
  • 如死则葬之于彼 - Conditional statement indicating that if the person dies, they should be buried in that place.
  • 其子不获已载去 - Phrase indicating that the son had no choice but to take the person away.
  • 得一磐石近池置之 - Description of placing a stone near a pond.
  • 悲泣而归 - Phrase describing returning home in sorrow and tears.
  • 其父忽见一黄犬来池中出没数四 - Description of the father suddenly seeing a yellow dog appearing and disappearing in the pond several times.
  • 状如沐浴 - Phrase describing the dog's actions as if it were bathing.
  • 既去其水即香 - Statement indicating that after the dog left, the water became fragrant.
  • 乃肘行而前 - Phrase describing the old man crawling forward on his elbows.
  • 既饮则觉四体稍轻 - Statement indicating that after drinking, the old man felt his body become slightly lighter.
  • 饮之不已即能坐 - Statement indicating that if the old man continued drinking, he could sit up.
  • 子惊喜乃复载归家 - Phrase describing the son's surprise and taking the old man back home.
  • 则能饮食不旬日而愈 - Statement indicating that the old man could eat and drink, and recovered within ten days.
  • 他日贾帅复出至前所置车处 - Phrase describing the general returning to the place where the carriage was previously placed.
  • 问曰前度病人在否 - Question asking if the previous patient was there.
  • 吏报今已平复 - Response indicating that the patient has now recovered.
  • 人病固有不可识者 - Statement indicating that some illnesses are difficult to recognize.
  • 此人是虱症世间无药可疗 - Diagnosis indicating that the person had a condition called '虱症' (lice disease), for which there was no medicine in the world.
  • 须得千年木梳烧灰服之 - Prescription indicating that a thousand-year-old comb, burned to ash, should be taken.
  • 不然即饮黄龙浴水 - Alternative prescription indicating that drinking water from the '黄龙浴' (Yellow Dragon Bath) would also work.
  • 此外无可治也不知何因而愈 - Statement indicating that there was no other treatment, and it was unknown how the person recovered.
  • 遣吏问之叟具以对 - Phrase describing the general sending someone to ask the old man, who answered in detail.
  • 此人天与其疾而自致其药命矣 - Statement indicating that the person's illness was given by heaven, and they found their own cure by fate.
  • 夫时人闻之咸服公之博识 - Statement indicating that people at the time admired the general's extensive knowledge.
  • 则医工所谓异人者信矣 - Statement indicating that the general was indeed a remarkable person, as medical practitioners would say.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贾帅 - Refers to a general or military leader named '贾' (Jia).
  • 虱症 - A term for a specific condition, possibly a skin disease or infestation.
  • 黄龙浴 - Refers to a specific type of water, possibly a spring or well with healing properties.
  • 千年木梳 - Refers to an ancient comb, symbolizing something rare and valuable.
  • 医工 - Refers to medical practitioners or doctors.
  • 异人 - Refers to a remarkable or extraordinary person.

Proper Nouns

  • 贾帅 - The name of a general or military leader.
  • 虱症 - A term for a specific condition.
  • 黄龙浴 - The name of a specific type of water.
  • 千年木梳 - The name of an ancient comb.
  • 医工 - The term for medical practitioners.
  • 异人 - The term for a remarkable person.

[1009]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 治针道士 - A Daoist who specializes in treating ailments with acupuncture.
  • 坠马伤足 - A literal expression meaning 'falling off a horse and injuring one's foot.'
  • 国医 - Refers to a state-licensed or highly skilled physician.
  • 针腿 - A literal expression meaning 'to needle the leg.'
  • 气如烟出 - Describes the appearance of qi (vital energy) exiting the body like smoke.
  • 渐困惫 - Means 'gradually becoming weak and exhausted.'
  • 将至不救 - Means 'on the verge of death and beyond help.'
  • 惶惧 - Means 'frightened and fearful.'
  • 诣门 - Means 'to visit the door' or 'to come to the gate.'
  • 合治 - Means 'to be able to treat' or 'to be able to cure.'
  • 视针处 - Means 'to examine the site of the needle.'
  • 何容易 - Means 'how easily' or 'how carelessly.'
  • 死生之穴 - Refers to the acupuncture points that can affect life and death.
  • 分毫 - Means 'a very small amount' or 'a slight difference.'
  • 血脉相通 - Means 'the blood vessels are interconnected.'
  • 江河 - A metaphor for the body's circulatory system.
  • 针灸在思其要津 - Means 'acupuncture requires careful consideration of the key points.'
  • 好手 - Means 'a skilled practitioner.'
  • 误中孔穴 - Means 'mistakenly hitting the wrong acupoint.'
  • 舁床 - Means 'to carry a bed' or 'to transport on a stretcher.'
  • 左腿气满处 - Means 'the left leg where qi is full.'
  • 拂然跃至檐板 - Means 'leapt out suddenly and flew to the eaves board.'
  • 泯然而合 - Means 'disappeared suddenly and healed.'
  • 拜谢 - Means 'to bow and thank.'
  • 金帛 - Refers to gold and silk, valuable gifts.
  • 啜茶一瓯 - Means 'to drink a bowl of tea.'
  • 竟不知所之矣 - Means 'eventually, no one knew where he had gone.'
  • 逸史 - Refers to a collection of anecdotes or stories from the past.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 朝士 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the Tang court.
  • 国医 - Refers to a highly skilled or state-licensed physician.
  • 道士 - Refers to a Daoist practitioner.
  • 针灸 - Refers to the traditional Chinese medical practice of acupuncture and moxibustion.
  • 气 - Refers to qi, the vital energy in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 逸史 - A collection of historical anecdotes and stories, often containing supernatural elements.

Proper Nouns

  • 朝士 - A high-ranking official in the Tang court.
  • 国医 - A highly skilled or state-licensed physician.
  • 道士 - A Daoist practitioner.
  • 逸史 - A collection of historical anecdotes and stories.

[1010]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 贞元末 - Refers to the end of the Zhenyuan era, a specific period in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 布衣 - Means 'a commoner' or 'an ordinary person,' often used to denote someone of low social status.
  • 游酒肆吟咏 - Describes the act of wandering in taverns and reciting poetry, indicating a lifestyle of leisure and literary pursuit.
  • 素秋 - A poetic term for autumn, often used in classical Chinese literature to evoke a sense of melancholy and reflection.
  • 万木凋落 - A vivid description of autumn's impact on nature, meaning 'ten thousand trees withering and falling.'
  • 长空寥廓 - Emphasizes the expansiveness of the autumn sky, meaning 'the vast sky is empty and boundless.'
  • 韶年 - A term for youth, often used in classical Chinese literature to contrast with old age.
  • 耄 - An ancient term for old age, often used in classical Chinese literature to describe the elderly.
  • 宛若春 - A poetic comparison to spring, often used to describe the beauty and vitality of youth.
  • 休吟春景与秋时 - A call to stop lamenting the changing seasons, often used in classical Chinese literature to encourage acceptance of life's cycles.
  • 争如且醉长安酒 - Suggests escaping reality through drinking, often used in classical Chinese literature to express a desire for temporary relief from life's burdens.
  • 荣华零悴 - Refers to the cyclical nature of life and fortune, often used in classical Chinese literature to reflect on the impermanence of human achievements.
  • 奚为 - A philosophical question about the purpose or meaning of life, often used in classical Chinese literature to provoke thought and reflection.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贞元 - The reign title of Emperor Dezong of Tang (779-805), a period marked by significant cultural and literary development.
  • 长安 - The ancient capital of China, known for its cultural and political importance.
  • 西蜀江边 - A location in the western part of China, specifically along the riverbank in Sichuan.

Proper Nouns

  • 贞元 - The reign title of Emperor Dezong of Tang (779-805).
  • 长安 - The ancient capital of China, known as Chang'an in modern times.

[1011]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 贞元末 - Refers to the late period of the reign of Emperor Dezong of the Tang Dynasty (780-805 CE).
  • 轻财好士 - Means 'frugal with wealth and fond of scholars,' describing a person who values scholars and is not wasteful with money.
  • 竹林防 - Likely a misreading or corruption of '竹林七贤' (Seven Sages of the Bamboo Grove), a famous group of scholars and poets from the Wei-Jin period.
  • 巧于体势 - Means 'skilled in form and posture,' referring to the technical skill of the painting.
  • 设薄伎 - Means 'to perform a small skill' or 'to demonstrate a minor talent.'
  • 不施五色 - Means 'without using colors,' referring to a monochrome or ink-only painting.
  • 精彩殊胜 - Means 'extraordinarily vivid and lifelike,' describing the effect of the painting.
  • 腾身赴图而灭 - Means 'leaped into the painting and disappeared,' a fantastical description of the artist's act.
  • 抵掌 - Means 'to clench fists' or 'to show excitement,' indicating a gesture of disbelief or challenge.
  • 绐 - Means 'to deceive' or 'to trick,' used here to express disbelief.
  • 三尺童子 - A colloquial term meaning 'a child of three feet tall,' a common way to refer to a young child.
  • 要其赌 - Means 'to challenge a bet.'
  • 五千抵负 - Means 'to wager five thousand units of currency.'
  • 保 - Means 'to guarantee' or 'to back.'
  • 他去 - Means 'to leave elsewhere,' indicating that the person departed without further ado.
  • 目击其事 - Means 'to witness the event with one's own eyes,' indicating firsthand knowledge.
  • 酉阳杂爼 - A collection of strange stories, likely the source of this anecdote.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贞元 - The era name of Emperor Dezong of the Tang Dynasty (780-805 CE).
  • 开州 - An ancient prefecture in Sichuan, modern-day Kaixian, Chongqing.
  • 宋存寿 - A person's name, likely a scholar or observer who witnessed the event.
  • 酉阳杂爼 - A collection of strange and fantastical stories compiled during the Tang Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 贞元 - The era name of Emperor Dezong of the Tang Dynasty (780-805 CE).
  • 开州 - An ancient prefecture in Sichuan, modern-day Kaixian, Chongqing.
  • 冉从长 - A person's name, likely a military officer or local leader.
  • 郭萱 - A person's name, one of the two scholars.
  • 栁城 - A person's name, the other scholar.
  • 宋存寿 - A person's name, likely a scholar or observer who witnessed the event.
  • 酉阳杂爼 - A collection of strange and fantastical stories compiled during the Tang Dynasty.

[1012]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 義師 - Refers to a righteous or just warrior, often with supernatural or divine qualities.
  • 狀如風狂 - Describes the appearance or behavior as if possessed by madness or frenzy.
  • 運斤壞其檐 - Literally 'wields an axe and destroys the eaves,' a metaphor for sudden, destructive action.
  • 禁之不止 - Means 'cannot be stopped even when restrained,' indicating the futility of restraint.
  • 弟子活计赖此 - Means 'my livelihood depends on this,' referring to the store owner's dependence on his business.
  • 无冬夏常积火烧幡木像悉火之 - Means 'no matter the season, there is always a fire that burns flags, wooden images, and statues,' indicating a perpetual, destructive force.
  • 好活烧鲤鱼不具汤而食 - Means 'he likes to roast live carp without boiling them first,' a peculiar and unusual eating habit.
  • 垢面不洗洗之輙雨其中以为雨 - Means 'he never washes his face, and when he does, it immediately rains, which he uses as a weather sign.'
  • 将死饮灰汁数斛乃念佛坐不复饮食 - Means 'on the verge of death, he drinks several liters of ash water, then recites Buddhist scriptures and stops eating and drinking,' indicating a ritualistic or spiritual preparation for death.
  • 百姓日观之坐七日而死时盛暑色不变支不摧 - Means 'the people watch him every day until he sits for seven days and dies in the heat, his appearance unchanged, and his body unbroken,' emphasizing the miraculous or supernatural nature of his death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 苏州贞元中 - Refers to the period of the Tang Dynasty during the reign of Emperor Dezong (779-805), a time of cultural and literary flourishing.
  • 废寺 - Refers to an abandoned temple, a common setting in classical Chinese literature for supernatural or mysterious events.
  • 义兴县 - Refers to a county in Jiangsu Province, known for its historical significance and cultural heritage.
  • 鳏夫 - Refers to a widower, a common figure in classical literature, often portrayed as lonely or pitiable.
  • 荆溪 - Refers to a river in Jiangsu Province, often associated with natural beauty and tranquility in classical poetry.
  • 白螺 - A white snail, possibly a symbol of purity or a supernatural entity in Chinese folklore.

Proper Nouns

  • 苏州 - The city of Suzhou, known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 贞元 - The reign title of Emperor Dezong of the Tang Dynasty (779-805).
  • 呉堪 - A name, likely referring to a historical or fictional figure, but not widely known in classical texts.
  • 荆溪 - A river in Jiangsu Province, often associated with natural beauty and tranquility in classical poetry.
  • 白螺 - A white snail, possibly a symbol of purity or a supernatural entity in Chinese folklore.

[1013]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 厨理爨堪自门而入 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Chef Li Cuan entered through the door.' '厨理' suggests a role or title, possibly 'chef' or 'kitchen supervisor.'
  • 其女遂归房 - Means 'his daughter then returned to her room.'
  • 不得堪拜之 - Ambiguous phrase that could mean 'he did not allow him to bow to her' or 'he could not bow to her.' The context suggests the former.
  • 天知君敬护泉源力勤小职 - Complex phrase that could be interpreted as 'Heaven knows you respect and protect the source of water, and you diligently perform your small duties.'
  • 哀君鳏独勅余以奉嫓 - Phrase that could mean 'Pity your loneliness, and I am ordered to serve you.'
  • 幸君垂悉无致疑阻 - Means 'May you understand and not cause doubt or obstruction.'
  • 堪敬而谢之 - Means 'Li Cuan respectfully thanked her.'
  • 自此弥将敬洽 - Means 'From then on, they became even more respectful and harmonious.'
  • 闾里传之颇增骇异 - Means 'The people of the neighborhood spread the story, and it greatly increased the sense of wonder and shock.'
  • 时县宰豪士闻堪美妻 - Means 'At that time, the county magistrate and the wealthy men heard about Li Cuan's beautiful wife.'
  • 因欲图之 - Means 'They then wanted to harm her.'
  • 堪为吏恭谨不犯笞责 - Means 'Li Cuan was an official who was respectful and cautious, and he never received corporal punishment.'
  • 宰谓堪曰 - Means 'The magistrate said to Li Cuan.'
  • 君熟于吏能久矣 - Means 'You are well-versed in administrative skills and have been doing this for a long time.'
  • 今要虾蟆毛及臂二物 - Means 'Now we need two items: the hair of a toad and the arm of a ghost.'
  • 晚衙须纳不应此物罪责非轻 - Means 'If you do not bring these items to the evening court, the punishment will not be light.'
  • 堪唯而走出 - Means 'Li Cuan nodded and left.'
  • 度人间无此物求不可得 - Means 'He thought that there were no such things in the world and that it would be impossible to find them.'
  • 颜色惨沮 - Means 'His face turned pale and gloomy.'
  • 归述于妻 - Means 'He told his wife.'
  • 吾今夕殒矣 - Means 'I will die tonight.'
  • 妻笑曰 - Means 'His wife laughed and said.'
  • 君忧余物不敢闻命 - Means 'You worry about the other things, but I am not afraid to obey your command.'
  • 二物之求妾能致矣 - Means 'I can obtain those two items.'
  • 堪闻言忧色稍解 - Means 'Li Cuan's worried expression slightly eased.'
  • 妻曰辞出取之少顷而到 - Means 'His wife said, 'I will go out and get them,' and she returned in a short while.'
  • 堪得以纳令 - Means 'Li Cuan was able to hand them over to the magistrate.'
  • 令视二物微笑曰且出 - Means 'The magistrate looked at the two items and smiled, saying, 'Let’s go.''
  • 然终欲害之 - Means 'But he still wanted to harm her.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 县宰 - Refers to the county magistrate, a local official in ancient China.
  • 豪士 - Refers to wealthy and influential men in the community.
  • 虾蟆毛及臂 - Refers to 'the hair of a toad and the arm of a ghost,' likely mythical or supernatural items, possibly symbolic or metaphorical.
  • 笞责 - Refers to corporal punishment, a common form of discipline in ancient China.
  • 晚衙 - Refers to the evening court session, a time when officials would gather to conduct business.

Proper Nouns

  • 厨理爨堪 - The name 'Li Cuan' is likely a proper noun, and '厨理' suggests a role or title, possibly 'chef' or 'kitchen supervisor.'
  • 县宰 - The title of the county magistrate.
  • 豪士 - Refers to wealthy and influential men in the community.

[1014]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 后一日又召堪曰 - Formal opening setting the scene.
  • 我要蜗斗一枚 - Desire for a specific object, possibly a local variant.
  • 君宜速觅此若不至祸在君矣 - Threatening statement using hyperbole.
  • 堪承命奔归 - Concise description of action.
  • 此蜗斗也 - Straightforward identification.
  • 能食火其粪火也 - Fantastical and metaphorical description.
  • 用此物奚为令除火埽粪方欲害堪 - Complex sentence with multiple clauses.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蜗斗 - Rare and possibly mythical object, possibly a local variant.
  • 宰 - Local official or magistrate.
  • 县 - Administrative region.

Proper Nouns

  • 宰 - Title of the local official, consistently rendered as 'the official' or 'the magistrate'.
  • 县 - Administrative region, consistently rendered as 'the county'.

[1015]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 困于名场 - Struggling in the competitive arena of seeking official positions.
  • 似得复落第 - Fluctuation between success and failure in exams.
  • 春景暄妍 - Poetic expression meaning 'spring scenery is bright and beautiful.'
  • 防蹇衞出都门 - With a slow and cautious escort out of the capital gate.
  • 贳酒一壶 - Borrowed a pot of wine, meaning purchased or obtained a pot of wine.
  • 借草而坐 - Sat on the grass, indicating a casual and relaxed posture.
  • 酣醉而寐 - Drank to intoxication and slept, describing a state of deep drunkenness.
  • 久之既觉 - After a long time, he woke up, indicating a prolonged period of sleep.
  • 因揖叙 - Then bowed and conversed, describing a polite interaction.
  • 余杯饮 - The remaining cup of wine, indicating the leftover drink.
  • 媿谢 - Felt ashamed and thanked, expressing a mix of embarrassment and gratitude.
  • 郎君萦悒 - Young sir is distressed, indicating the young man's emotional state.
  • 宁要知前事邪 - Do you want to know future events?, expressing a question about fortune-telling.
  • 应举已久 - Has been taking exams for a long time, indicating prolonged efforts in the imperial examinations.
  • 有一第分乎 - Do you have a chance to pass?, asking about the likelihood of success.
  • 大有事 - There are many things, indicating a complex situation.
  • 更问 - Ask further, suggesting a continuation of the conversation.
  • 亷察乎 - Will you become a censor?, referring to a specific official position.
  • 天子真者即不得假者即得 - If it's the true emperor you can't get, but if it's a false one you can, a cryptic statement about political power.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 苗晋卿 - A historical figure, a prominent statesman of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 义宁坊狂人 - Refers to a madman in the Yining Ward, possibly a local legend or anecdote.
  • 张俨 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 奚乐山 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 王居士 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 俞叟 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 衡岳道人 - Refers to a Taoist hermit living on Mount Heng.
  • 李业 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 石旻 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 管涔山隠者 - Refers to a hermit living in the Guancen Mountains.
  • 宋师儒 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 防昌狂士 - Refers to a madman from Fangchang, possibly a local legend or anecdote.
  • 唐庆 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 卢钧 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.

Proper Nouns

  • 苗晋卿 - A historical figure, a prominent statesman of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 义宁坊 - A ward in Chang'an (modern-day Xi'an), the capital of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 张俨 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 奚乐山 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 王居士 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 俞叟 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 衡岳 - Mount Heng, one of the Five Great Mountains of China.
  • 李业 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 石旻 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 管涔山 - Guancen Mountains, a mountain range in Shanxi Province.
  • 宋师儒 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 防昌 - A place name, possibly a village or town in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 唐庆 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.
  • 卢钧 - A historical figure, possibly a contemporary of the text's author.

[1016]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 元和初 - Refers to the early years of the Yuanhe era, a period of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 上都 - Refers to the capital city of Chang'an during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 義寧坊 - The name of a district in Chang'an.
  • 風狂 - Refers to a state of madness or insanity.
  • 俗呼為五娘 - Commonly called Five-Niang, indicating a nickname.
  • 常止宿於永穆牆下 - Often stayed under the wall of Yongmu, suggesting a habitual behavior.
  • 中使 - Refers to an imperial envoy or messenger.
  • 茹大夫 - The name of a specific imperial envoy.
  • 金陵 - Refers to Nanjing, a city in southern China.
  • 狂者眾 - Refers to a group of madmen.
  • 名之信夫 - Named it Xinfu, indicating a nickname.
  • 或歌或哭 - Sometimes sang, sometimes cried, describing erratic behavior.
  • 往往驗未來事 - Often predicted future events, indicating prophetic abilities.
  • 盛暑擁絮 - In the heat of summer, wrapped in cotton, describing an unusual behavior.
  • 未嘗沾汗 - Never sweating, indicating an unusual physical state.
  • 沍寒袒露體無跔坼 - In the cold, exposed, with no cracks in the skin, describing another unusual physical state.
  • 信夫忽扣馬 - Xinfu suddenly tapped the horse, indicating an unexpected action.
  • 我有妹五娘在城 - I have a sister named Five-Niang in the city, indicating a familial relationship.
  • 有少信必為我逹也 - If there is any message, please deliver it for me, indicating a request.
  • 中使素知其異 - The imperial envoy had always known of his oddities, indicating prior knowledge.
  • 欣然許之 - Agreed happily, indicating a positive response.
  • 探懷中一襆納中使靴中 - Reached into his chest for a bundle and placed it in the imperial envoy's boot, indicating a secretive action.
  • 謂語五娘無事速歸也 - Tell Five-Niang to return quickly if there's no news, indicating a message.
  • 長樂坡 - The name of a place.
  • 五娘已至 - Five-Niang had already arrived, indicating a quick arrival.
  • 笑曰 - Smilingly said, indicating a joyful tone.
  • 我兄有信大夫可見還 - My brother has a message, please see him, indicating a request.
  • 遽取信授之 - Quickly took the message and gave it to her, indicating a swift action.
  • 因發襆有衣三事 - Unwrapped the bundle to find three pieces of clothing, indicating the contents.
  • 乃衣之而舞 - Put them on and danced, indicating a celebratory action.
  • 大笑而歸 - Laughed loudly and returned, indicating a joyful return.
  • 復至牆下一夕而死 - Returned to the wall and died the next night, indicating a tragic end.
  • 其坊率錢葬之 - The district collected money to bury her, indicating a communal effort.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 倍常功固不能多办矣 - If one doubles the normal work, it cannot be done more.
  • 所请皆依乐山乃闭户屏人丁丁不辍 - All requests were made according to Lè Shān, who then closed the door and isolated himself, working diligently without pause.
  • 及晓啓主人曰并已毕矣愿受六十缗而去也 - By dawn, he opened the door and told the master that everything was finished, and he wished to receive sixty strings of cash and leave.
  • 主人洎邻里大竒之 - The master and the neighbors were greatly surprised.
  • 则视所为精妙锱铢无失 - Then they saw that his work was exquisite, with not a single error in the smallest detail.
  • 众共惊骇即付其钱乐山谢辞而去 - Everyone was amazed, and immediately paid him the money. Lè Shān thanked them and left.
  • 主人密所之 - The master secretly followed him.
  • 其时严雪累日都下薪米翔贵 - At that time, heavy snow had fallen for several days, and the price of firewood and rice in the capital had risen sharply.
  • 乐山遂以所得遍散与寒乞贫窭不能自振之徒 - Lè Shān then distributed all his earnings among the poor and beggars who could not support themselves.
  • 俄顷而尽遂南出都城不复得而见矣 - In a short while, it was all gone, and he left the capital to the south, never to be seen again.
  • 常持珠诵佛施药里巷家属十余口丰俭适中 - He often held a rosary, recited the Buddha's name, and distributed medicine. He had more than ten family members living in the neighborhood, and their living standards were moderate.
  • 一日游终南山之灵应台台有观音殿基 - One day, he visited the Lingying Terrace on Zhongnan Mountain, where the foundation of a Guanyin temple was located.
  • 询其僧则曰梁栋栾栌悉已具矣属山路险峻辇负上下大役工徒非三百缗不可集事 - He asked the monk, who replied that the beams, pillars, and other wooden structures were all ready, but the mountain path was dangerous, and transporting materials up and down required a large number of workers, costing no less than three hundred strings of cash.
  • 居士许诺期旬日赍镪而至入京乃托于人曰有富室危病医药不救者某能活之得三百千则成南山佛屋矣 - The monk agreed and promised to return within ten days. He brought the money to the capital and entrusted someone to say that there was a wealthy family with a critically ill member who could not be saved by medicine, and he could cure them for three hundred strings of cash, which would be used to build the temple on Zhongnan Mountain.
  • 果有延寿坊鬻金银珠玉者女嵗十五遘病甚危众医拱手不能措愿以其价疗之 - Indeed, there was a family in the Yanshou Ward that sold gold, silver, and precious stones. They had a fifteen-year-old daughter who fell seriously ill, and all the doctors could do was stand by helplessly. They wished to use the money to treat her.
  • 居士则设盟于牋期之必效且曰滞工役已久今留神丹不足多虑某先驰此镪付所主僧冀获双济 - The monk then made an oath on a note, promising to heal her certainly, and said that since the construction work had been delayed for a long time, he would not need to worry about the elixir. He first sent the money to the temple's abbot, hoping to achieve both goals.
  • 鬻金者亦奉释教因许之留丹于小壶中赍缗而往渉旬无耗女则物化其家始营哀具 - The family that sold gold also followed Buddhism and agreed. The monk left the elixir in a small bottle and took the money to the family. After ten days, there was no news. The girl died, and her family began preparing for her funeral.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乃命密一室焚槐栁之润者涌烟于其间人不可迩中平一榻借尸其上褫药数粒杂置于顶鼻中又以铜器贮温水置于心上则墐户屏众伺之及晓烟尽薫黔其室 - Then ordered a room to be made secret; burned the resinous parts of the locust and willow trees (unclear); smoke rose in the room; no one could get close; a flat mat in the middle; used the corpse on it; took out several pills; mixed and placed in the nostrils; also placed a copper container with warm water on the chest; then sealed the door and kept the crowd away, waiting; by dawn, the smoke had dissipated; the room was blackened by smoke.
  • 居士染指于水曰尚可救亟命取乳碎丹数粒滴于唇吻俄顷流入口中喜曰无忧矣则以纎纩防其鼻复以温水置于心及夜又执烛以俟铜壶下漏数刻鼻纩微嘘又数刻心水微灎则以前药复滴于鼻须臾忽黎明胎息续矣 - The lay practitioner dipped his finger in the water; said it can still be saved; quickly ordered to take crushed pills and mix with milk; dropped on the lips; soon flowed into the mouth; gladly said there is no need to worry; then used fine cotton to block the nose; again placed warm water on the chest; by night, held a candle and waited; the copper pot dripped for several hours; the cotton in the nose slightly exhaled; after several more hours, the water on the chest slightly moved; then used the previous medicine and dropped it in the nose; soon, suddenly; at dawn, breathing resumed.
  • 一家惊异媿谢王生生乃更留药而去或许再来竟不复至后移家他适不知所从女适人育数子而卒 - The family was surprised; felt ashamed and thanked; unclear '王生生' (likely a name or title); then left more medicine and went away; perhaps he would come again; but never returned; later moved to another place; no one knows where he went; the daughter married and had several children before dying.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 衡岳西原近朱陵洞 - Describes the geographical location, 'West Plain of Mount Heng near the Zhu Ling Cave.'
  • 其山崄絶多大木猛兽 - Describes the mountain's dangerous terrain with large trees and fierce beasts.
  • 人到者率迷路或遇巨蛇不得进 - Means 'Those who come here often get lost or encounter large snakes and cannot proceed.'
  • 长庆中有头陁悟空常裹粮持锡夜入山林 - Refers to a monk named Wukong who often carried food and a staff and entered the mountains at night during the Longqing period.
  • 越尸侵虎初无所惧 - Means 'He crossed over corpses and approached tigers without any fear.'
  • 至朱陵原游览累日 - Means 'He arrived at Zhu Ling Plain and toured there for several days.'
  • 扪萝垂踵无幽不迹 - Means 'He climbed vines and walked barefoot, exploring every hidden path.'
  • 因是趼拆憩于岩下 - Means 'Because of this, his feet were cracked, and he rested under a rock.'
  • 长吁曰饥渇如此不遇主人 - Means 'He sighed and said, 'I am so hungry and thirsty, yet I have not met the host.''
  • 忽见前岩有道士坐绳牀 - Means 'Suddenly, he saw a Taoist sitting on a rope bed in front of a rock.'
  • 僧诣之不动遂责其无宾主意 - Means 'The monk went to him, but the Taoist did not move, so the monk blamed him for not showing hospitality.'
  • 复告以饥困 - Means 'He then told the Taoist about his hunger and fatigue.'
  • 道士欻起指石地曰此有米及镢斸石深数寸 - Means 'The Taoist suddenly stood up and pointed to the stone ground, saying, 'There is rice here; dig the stone a few inches deep.''
  • 令僧探之得陈米斗余即置于釡承瀑水敲火煑饭 - Means 'He instructed the monk to dig and found some old rice, which he then placed in a pot, heated by a waterfall and fire, to cook rice.'
  • 观僧食一口未尽辞以未熟 - Means 'The monk ate a mouthful but said it was not cooked.'
  • 道士笑曰君飡止此可谓薄食我当毕之 - Means 'The Taoist laughed and said, 'You eat only this much, which is a thin meal; I will finish it.''
  • 遂吃硬饭又曰我为客设戏乃处木褭枝投盖危石猿悬鸟眞防闪目有顷又旋绕绳牀转甚急但覩衣色成规倐忽失所 - Means 'He then ate the hard rice and said, 'I have prepared a game for the guest. He climbed a tree branch and threw it over a dangerous rock, like a monkey hanging and a bird flying, truly dazzling to the eyes. For a moment, he spun around the rope bed very quickly, but only saw the color of his clothes forming a circle, suddenly disappearing.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 衡岳 - Refers to Mount Heng, one of the Five Great Mountains in China.

  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist practitioner.
  • 绳牀 - A type of bed or seat used in Taoist practices.

  • 敲火 - Refers to the method of making fire by striking stones together.
  • 硬饭 - Refers to hard, uncooked rice.
  • 木褭枝 - Refers to a tree branch.
  • 危石 - Refers to a dangerous rock.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 曰嘻此即神告也 - Formal expression meaning 'Alas, this is the divine message.' '曰嘻' is an interjection expressing surprise or awe, and '神告' refers to a divine revelation.
  • 夫将行而失其鞋是事皆不谐矣 - Concessive statement meaning 'If one is about to leave and loses their shoes, all matters will not go well.' '夫将行而失其鞋' is a conditional clause, and '是事皆不谐矣' is the result clause.
  • 非徒如此而已也 - Rhetorical formula meaning 'Not only is this the case, but...' It emphasizes that the situation is more severe than initially stated.
  • 京国将有乱当不可复振 - Prophetic statement meaning 'The capital will face turmoil that cannot be revived.' '京国' refers to the capital city, and '不可复振' means 'cannot be revived' or 'cannot be restored.'
  • 君当百艰备歴然无忧也 - Statement of reassurance meaning 'You will face many hardships but will have no worries.' '百艰备歴' means 'many hardships to be endured,' and '无忧也' means 'no worries.'
  • 子之爵禄皆在江淮官当至门下侍郎 - Statement of destiny meaning 'Your rank and salary will be in Jianghuai, and your official position will reach that of Menxia Shilang.' '爵禄' refers to rank and salary, and '门下侍郎' is a high-ranking official position.
  • 彦伯因思之江淮安得有门下侍郎遂行至长安 - Narrative statement meaning 'Yanbo thought about it and wondered how Jianghuai could have a Menxia Shilang, so he traveled to Chang'an.' '因思之' means 'thought about it,' and '遂行至长安' means 'so he traveled to Chang'an.'
  • 适防大驾西幸随至岐陇梁冦围城三年 - Narrative statement meaning 'He just happened to meet the emperor's western journey and followed him to Qilong, where the Liang bandits besieged the city for three years.' '适防大驾西幸' means 'just happened to meet the emperor's western journey,' and '随至岐陇' means 'followed him to Qilong.'
  • 彦伯辛苦备至驾既出城彦伯逃还吉州刺史彭珍厚遇之 - Narrative statement meaning 'Yanbo endured great hardship. After the emperor left the city, Yanbo fled back to Jizhou, where Governor Peng Zhen treated him kindly.' '辛苦备至' means 'endured great hardship,' and '厚遇之' means 'treated him kindly.'
  • 累摄县邑伪吴平江西复见选用登朝至户部侍郎 - Narrative statement meaning 'He was repeatedly appointed to county positions. After the fall of the false Wu state, Jiangxi was reappointed, and he was selected for court service, reaching the position of Minister of Revenue.' '累摄县邑' means 'repeatedly appointed to county positions,' and '登朝至户部侍郎' means 'reached the position of Minister of Revenue.'
  • 防临轩策命齐王彦伯摄门下侍郎行事既受命思店妪之言大不悦数月遂卒 - Narrative statement meaning 'When the emperor personally appointed Prince Qi as Menxia Shilang, Yanbo was appointed to act as Menxia Shilang. After receiving the appointment, he thought about the storekeeper's words and was greatly displeased, dying several months later.' '防临轩策命' means 'personally appointed,' and '遂卒' means 'died.'

'状貌竒伟' means 'appearance was extraordinary,' '裸袒而通身有毛' means 'naked with hair covering the entire body,' '自言商贾也' means 'he said he was a merchant,' '顷嵗泛舟至此' means 'a few years ago, he sailed here,' '遇风举家没溺而身独得就岸' means 'encountered a storm and the whole family drowned, but he alone reached the shore,' '数日食草根饮涧水因得不死' means 'for several days, he ate grass roots and drank stream water and thus survived,' '嵗余身乃生毛自尔' means 'after a year, his body grew hair and remained so.'

  • 不惮疲苦 - Idiom meaning 'not afraid of fatigue and hardship'; describes the person's undaunted spirit.
  • 冒涉流沙 - Idiom meaning 'braving the shifting sands'; describes the person's journey across the desert.
  • 甚加赏接 - Idiom meaning 'highly praised and warmly received'; describes the emperor's reception of the person.
  • 立精舍 - Idiom meaning 'established a temple' or 'set up a place of study'; describes the creation of a place for the person.
  • 汉地有沙门之始也 - Idiom marking the beginning of Buddhism in China; '沙门' refers to a Buddhist monk.
  • 蕴其深解无所宣述 - Idiom meaning 'had profound understanding but did not explain or teach it'; describes the person's knowledge and reticence.
  • 少时卒于雒阳 - Idiom meaning 'died in Luoyang after a short time'; describes the person's death.
  • 腾译四十二章经一卷 - Idiom meaning 'translated a volume of the Forty-Two Chapters Sutra'; describes the person's translation work.
  • 初缄在兰台石室第十四间中 - Idiom meaning 'initially kept in the fourteenth room of the Stone Chamber of the Lan Tai'; describes the storage of the translation.
  • 腾所住处 - Idiom meaning 'the place where the person lived'; describes the person's residence.
  • 白马寺是也 - Idiom meaning 'the White Horse Temple'; describes the temple where the person lived.
  • 外夷国王尝毁破诸寺 - Idiom meaning 'a foreign king once destroyed many temples'; describes the destruction of temples.
  • 唯招提寺未及毁壊 - Idiom meaning 'only the Zhaoti Temple was not destroyed'; describes the survival of one temple.
  • 夜有一白马绕塔悲鸣 - Idiom meaning 'at night, a white horse circled the pagoda and mourned'; describes a supernatural event.
  • 即以啓王 - Idiom meaning 'immediately informed the king'; describes the reporting of the event.
  • 王即停壊诸寺 - Idiom meaning 'the king stopped destroying the temples'; describes the king's reaction.
  • 因改招提以为白马 - Idiom meaning 'renamed the Zhaoti as the White Horse Temple'; describes the renaming of the temple.
  • 诸寺立名多取则焉 - Idiom meaning 'many temples took their names from the White Horse Temple'; describes the influence of the temple's name.
  • 自言诵经论数万章 - Idiom meaning 'claimed to have memorized tens of thousands of chapters of scriptures'; describes the person's claim.
  • 为天竺学者之师 - Idiom meaning 'was a teacher of scholars in India'; describes the person's role.
  • 时蔡愔既至彼国 - Idiom meaning 'at that time, Cai Yin had already arrived in that country'; describes the timing of Cai Yin's arrival.
  • 遂相随而来 - Idiom meaning 'came together'; describes the joint arrival.
  • 防彼学徒留碍 - Idiom meaning 'hindered by the students'; describes the obstacle faced.
  • 兰乃间行而至之 - Idiom meaning 'Zhuban secretly traveled to the place'; describes the secret journey.
  • 既达雒阳与腾同歩时 - Idiom meaning 'after reaching Luoyang, walked with Teng at the same time'; describes the meeting.
  • 便善汉言 - Idiom meaning 'became skilled in the Chinese language'; describes the person's language skills.
  • 愔于西域获经即为翻译 - Idiom meaning 'Cai Yin obtained scriptures in the Western Regions and immediately translated them'; describes the translation work.
  • 所谓十地断结佛本僧法海藏佛本行四十二章等五部 - Idiom listing several Buddhist texts; '所谓' means 'so-called' and the list includes the Ten Grounds, the Cutting Off of Bonds, the Buddha's Essence, the Monastic Law, the Sea of the Dharma, the Buddha's Conduct, and the Forty-Two Chapters, among others.
  • 江左唯四十二章经今见在 - Idiom meaning 'in the Eastern Jin, only the Forty-Two Chapters Sutra is still in existence'; describes the survival of one text.
  • 可二千余言 - Idiom meaning 'about two thousand words'; describes the length of the text.
  • 汉地见 - Idiom meaning 'seen in China'; describes the presence of the text.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 汉地 - Refers to China, specifically the Han Dynasty.
  • 明帝 - Refers to Emperor Ming of Han, who ruled from 57 to 75 AD.
  • 雒邑 - Refers to Luoyang, the capital of the Eastern Han Dynasty.
  • 沙门 - Refers to a Buddhist monk.
  • 兰台石室 - Refers to the Stone Chamber of the Lan Tai, a repository for important documents and texts.
  • 白马寺 - Refers to the White Horse Temple in Luoyang, one of the earliest Buddhist temples in China.
  • 竺法兰 - Refers to Zhuban, a Buddhist monk from Central India.
  • 摩腾 - Refers to Mahakasyapa, a Buddhist monk who is said to have brought Buddhism to China.
  • 蔡愔 - Refers to Cai Yin, a Chinese envoy sent to the Western Regions to invite Buddhist monks to China.

[1055]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 存诸经唯此为始也 - Formal statement meaning 'Of all the scriptures, this one is the beginning.'
  • 愔又于西域得画释迦倚像是优田王防檀像师第四作 - Detailed phrase referring to the acquisition of a painting of the Buddha by a particular artist.
  • 既至雒阳明帝即令画工图写置清凉台中及显节陵上 - Describes actions taken by Emperor Ming after the artist arrived in Luoyang.
  • 旧像今不复存焉 - Statement indicating that the old images no longer exist.
  • 可问西域梵人 - Suggests consulting people from the Western Regions who understand Sanskrit.
  • 后法兰既至众人追问之 - Refers to the arrival of a person named法兰 and the subsequent questioning by others.
  • 兰云世界终尽刼火洞烧此灰是也 - Statement about the nature of the black ash found in the lake, which is said to be the result of a cosmic conflagration.
  • 朔言有徴信者甚众 - Indicates that there were many who believed in the accuracy of what东方朔 said.
  • 兰后卒于雒阳春秋六十余矣 - States that法兰 died in Luoyang at the age of over sixty.
  • 康僧防其先康居国人世居天竺 - Describes康僧防's ancestry and residence.
  • 其父因商贾移于交趾 - Explains that his father moved to Jiaozhi due to trade.
  • 防年十余嵗二亲并亡以至性奉孝服毕出家厉行甚峻 - Describes康僧防's early life and his decision to become a monk.
  • 辨于枢机颇属文翰 - Indicates his skill in rhetoric and writing.
  • 时孙权已制江右而佛教未行 - Contextualizes the time period and the spread of Buddhism.
  • 初汉桓灵之世有支防悔出众经有支亮字绝明亮学于防谦又受业于亮傅览经籍莫不谙究世间伎艺多所综习遍学异书通六国语 - Provides background on支防悔 and支亮, and their contributions to Buddhism.
  • 汉末遇乱避地于吴孙权闻其才慧召见之日拜为博士使辅导东宫与韦曜诸人共尽匡益但生自外域故 - Details支谦的遭遇和他在吴国的地位.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大教 - Refers to the Great Teaching or the Buddhist Dharma.
  • 梵文 - Refers to Sanskrit, the language of many Buddhist texts.
  • 翻译已妙 - Means 'translation has already been exquisite' or 'translation has been done beautifully.'
  • 善方言 - Means 'good at local dialects' or 'proficient in local languages.'
  • 集众本译为汉文 - Means 'to gather various versions and translate them into Chinese.'
  • 黄武元年 - Refers to the first year of the Huangwu era of the Wu state during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 建兴中 - Refers to the middle period of the Jianxing era of the Shu Han state during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 维摩大般若泥洹法句瑞应本起等四十九经 - Refers to forty-nine scriptures including the Vimalakirti Sutra, the Great Prajnaparamita Sutra, the Nirvana Sutra, the Dharmapada, the Lotus Sutra, and the Pure Land Sutras.
  • 圣仪 - Refers to the sacred rites or rituals.
  • 辞防文雅 - Means 'words are elegant and refined.'
  • 菩萨连句梵呗三契并注 - Refers to three chants for bodhisattvas, with annotations.
  • 了本先死经等 - Refers to the *Lüben Xiandie Jing* (a text on the precepts and death).
  • 行于世 - Means 'circulated in the world' or 'became widely known.'
  • 吴地初染大法风化未全 - Means 'in Wu, the region first came into contact with the Great Law, but the cultural transformation was not yet complete.'
  • 僧防 - Refers to the Buddhist monk and translator Kumarajiva (鸠摩罗什).
  • 道振江左 - Means 'to revitalize the Way in the Jiangnan region.'
  • 兴立图寺 - Means 'to build temples.'
  • 赤乌十年 - Refers to the tenth year of the Chitu era of the Wu state during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 建业 - Refers to Jianye, the capital of the Wu state.
  • 营立茅茨 - Means 'to build thatched huts.'
  • 设像行道 - Means 'to set up images and practice the Way.'
  • 初见沙门服形未及其道 - Means 'the appearance of the sastras (Buddhist monks) was not yet fully recognized as the Way.'
  • 疑为矫异 - Means 'suspected as false or strange.'
  • 有司奏曰 - Means 'the officials reported.'
  • 异人入境 - Means 'strange people entering the country.'
  • 自称沙门 - Means 'claiming to be a sastra.'
  • 容服非恒 - Means 'appearance and attire were not common.'
  • 事应察检 - Means 'the matter should be investigated.'
  • 权曰 - Refers to Sun Quan, the ruler of the Wu state.
  • 汉明梦神 - Refers to Emperor Ming of Han dreaming of a golden deity.
  • 彼之所事 - Means 'what they worship.'
  • 岂其遗风耶 - Means 'is it not a remnant of their tradition?'
  • 即召会诘问 - Means 'he immediately summoned them for questioning.'
  • 有何灵验 - Means 'what miraculous signs do you have?'
  • 防曰 - Refers to Kumarajiva's response.
  • 如来迁迹忽逾千载 - Means 'the Tathagata's footsteps have suddenly passed over a thousand years.'
  • 遗骨舍利 - Refers to relics of the Buddha.
  • 神曜无方 - Means 'divine radiance is boundless.'
  • 昔阿育王起塔及八 - Refers to the construction of stupas by Emperor Ashoka.

[1057]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遗化 - Refers to the spiritual or moral transformation left behind by a previous era or individual.
  • 夸诞 - Means exaggerated or fantastic.
  • 舍利 - Relics of the Buddha, often in the form of bones or teeth, which are considered sacred in Buddhism.
  • 希有之瑞 - Rare and auspicious, referring to a rare and blessed sign.
  • 舍利威神 - Refers to the supernatural power of the relics.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 万四千夫塔寺 - Refers to the Wanshisi temple, a significant Buddhist temple.
  • 宣尼 - Refers to Confucius, often addressed as 'Master Xuan' in classical Chinese texts.
  • 文王 - Refers to King Wen of Zhou, a revered figure in Chinese history and culture.
  • 文不在兹乎 - A quote from the Analects, meaning 'Is culture not in this place?'
  • 铜盘 - A copper dish, often used in rituals and ceremonies.

Proper Nouns

  • 权 - A person or authority figure, possibly a government official or religious leader.
  • 防 - A person's name, likely a monk or spiritual leader.

[1058]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 金刚之杵 - Metaphorical term symbolizing the indestructibility of the Buddha's teachings.
  • 不能碎 - Meaning 'cannot break' or 'cannot shatter'.
  • 权命令试之 - The authority ordered a test to be conducted.
  • 防更誓曰 - The monk then took an oath and said.
  • 法云方被苍生仰泽 - The Dharma rain is just beginning to benefit the masses.
  • 愿更垂神迹以广示威灵 - May further miraculous signs be shown to demonstrate the power and presence.
  • 砧磓俱防 - The anvil and hammer both shattered.
  • 舍利无损 - The relics remained undamaged.
  • 权大嗟伏 - The authority greatly marveled and submitted.
  • 即为建塔 - Immediately built a stupa.
  • 始有佛寺故号建初寺 - Since this was the first Buddhist temple, it was named Jianchu Temple.
  • 因名其地为陁里 - The place was named Tolo (the place where the stupa is located).
  • 由是江左大法遂兴 - Thus, Buddhism in the Jiangnan region began to flourish.
  • 至孙皓即位 - When Sun Hao ascended the throne.
  • 法令苛虐 - The laws were harsh and cruel.
  • 废弃淫祠 - Abandoned the temples of false gods.
  • 及佛寺并欲毁壊 - And also wanted to destroy the Buddhist temples.
  • 皓曰此又何由而兴 - Sun Hao said, 'How could this have arisen?'
  • 若其义教贞正与圣典相应者当存奉其道 - If the teachings are virtuous and in accordance with the sacred texts, they should be preserved and followed.
  • 如其无实皆悉焚之 - If they are not genuine, they should all be burned.
  • 诸臣佥曰 - The ministers all said.
  • 佛之威力不同于神 - The power of the Buddha is different from that of the gods.
  • 康防感瑞 - Kang Senghui felt the auspicious signs.
  • 大皇创寺 - The great king founded the temple.
  • 今若轻毁贻后悔 - Now if we lightly destroy it, we will regret it.
  • 皓遣张昱诣寺诘防 - Sun Hao sent Zhang Yu to the temple to question the monk.
  • 昱雅有才辨 - Zhang Yu had great talent and eloquence.
  • 难问纵横 - He asked difficult questions in a wide-ranging manner.
  • 防应机骋辞 - The monk responded with skillful and eloquent words.
  • 文理锋出自旦之夕 - His arguments were sharp and logical, from morning till night.
  • 昱不能屈 - Zhang Yu could not defeat him.
  • 既退防送于门 - Afterward, the monk escorted him to the door.
  • 时寺侧有淫祠在 - At that time, there was a temple of false gods beside the temple.
  • 昱曰化既敷此辈何故近而不革 - Zhang Yu said, 'Since the true teachings have spread, why do these temples remain nearby without being changed?'
  • 防曰震霆破山聋 - The monk replied, 'The thunderbolt breaks the deafness of the mountain.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金刚之杵 - A symbol of the indestructibility of the Buddha's teachings.
  • 权 - Refers to a high-ranking official or authority figure.
  • 舍利 - Relics of the Buddha, often considered sacred.
  • 建初寺 - The first Buddhist temple in the Jiangnan region.
  • 孙皓 - A ruler of the Eastern Wu kingdom during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 淫祠 - Temples dedicated to false or non-Buddhist deities.
  • 康防 - A reference to Kang Senghui, a prominent Buddhist monk who played a significant role in the spread of Buddhism in China.
  • 张昱 - A minister sent by Sun Hao to question the monk.

[1059]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 萬里懸應 - Resonate from a distance of ten thousand miles; suggests a profound and far-reaching effect.
  • 肝膽楚越 - Metaphor for being as far apart as Chu and Yue, symbolizing deep estrangement or conflict.
  • 周孔所言略示近迹 - Confucius and Zhou spoke only to indicate the immediate traces; emphasizes the limited scope of their teachings.
  • 至於釋教則備極幽防 - As for the Buddhist teachings, they are fully detailed and profound; highlights the depth and comprehensiveness of Buddhist doctrine.
  • 故行惡則有地獄長苦修善則有天宮永樂 - Therefore, those who do evil will suffer in hell forever, while those who do good will enjoy eternal bliss in heaven; underscores the moral and spiritual consequences of actions.
  • 舉兹以明勸沮 - This is done to encourage and discourage; explains the purpose of the teachings.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 周孔 - Refers to Confucius and Zhou, representing the teachings of Confucianism.
  • 釋教 - Refers to Buddhism.
  • 地獄 - Refers to hell, a concept in both Chinese and Buddhist beliefs.
  • 天宮 - Refers to heaven, a concept in both Chinese and Buddhist beliefs.
  • 皓 - Likely refers to a historical figure, possibly a ruler or a notable person, but more context is needed to identify him accurately.
  • 防 - Likely refers to a scholar or advisor, but more context is needed to identify him accurately.
  • 宿衛兵 - Refers to palace guards, responsible for the security of the imperial palace.
  • 後宮 - Refers to the inner palace, where the emperor's concubines and other female attendants lived.

  • 皓 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical figure.
  • 防 - A proper name, likely referring to a scholar or advisor.
  • 周孔 - Proper names, referring to Confucius and Zhou.
  • 釋教 - A term for Buddhism.
  • 地獄 - A term for hell.
  • 天宮 - A term for heaven.
  • 宿衛兵 - A term for palace guards.
  • 後宮 - A term for the inner palace.

[1060]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 園于地得一金像 - Literal phrase meaning 'found a golden statue in the garden.'
  • 呈皓皓使着不净处以汁灌之 - 'Presented it to Emperor Sun Hao, who placed it in an unclean place and sprinkled it with dirty water.'
  • 共诸羣臣笑以为乐 - 'Together with the ministers, they laughed and took it as entertainment.'
  • 呌呼彻天 - 'He cried out so loudly that it reached the sky.'

[1061]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 译出众经 - Translated from various sutras or rendered from the many scriptures. The verb *译* means 'to translate,' and *出众经* refers to the many canonical Buddhist scriptures.
  • 并妙得经体文义允正 - Skillfully grasped the essence of the scriptures, the meaning of the text, and the proper interpretation. *妙得* means 'skillfully obtained'; *经体* refers to the essence or structure of the scripture; *文义允正* means 'the textual meaning is accurate and proper.'
  • 传泥洹呗声清摩哀亮一代模式 - Transmitted the *Nihuan Bei* (Nirvana chant) with clear, resonant, mournful, and bright tones, setting a model for one generation. *呗* refers to Buddhist chanting or recitation; *泥洹* is a transliteration of *Nirvana*.
  • 又注安般守意法竟道树等三经并制经序辞趣雅俊义防防宻并见行于世 - Also annotated the *Anban Shouyi Fa* (Breathing and Mindfulness Method), the *Dao Shu* (Bodhi Tree) and other three sutras, and composed prefaces for the scriptures. The language is elegant and refined, the meaning profound and secret, and these works are now in circulation. *安般守意法* is likely a reference to the *Anapanasati Sutta* or similar breathing meditation texts.
  • 若必目覩所不能耳言竟塔即出五色光照耀堂刹诱肃然毛由此敬信于寺东更立小塔 - If I could see it with my own eyes, I would not doubt it with my ears. When he spoke, the tower immediately emitted five-colored light, illuminating the hall and the stupa. Zhao You was startled and felt his hair stand on end. From this, he became respectful and believed in the Dharma, and built a small tower to the east of the temple. *毛* is a character that likely represents 'hair standing on end' or a sudden shock; *堂刹* refers to the temple hall and stupa.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 阿难念弥镜面王察微王梵皇经等 - These are likely names of specific Buddhist sutras. *阿难* is Ananda, the Buddha's cousin and a prominent disciple. *镜面王* may be a transliteration of *Jingmian Wang*, possibly a title or deity. *察微王* and *梵皇* also appear to be titles of Buddhist kings or deities. *梵皇* may refer to the 'Brahma King,' a deity in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 小品及六度集杂譬喻等经 - These are titles of Buddhist texts: *Xiao Pin* (Small Treatise), *Liu Du Ji* (Collection of the Six Perfections), and *Za Biyu* (Miscellaneous Analogies). These are part of the Buddhist canon.
  • 安般守意法 - This is likely a reference to the *Anapanasati Sutta* (Breathing Meditation Sutta), a key text in early Buddhist meditation practice.
  • 泥洹呗 - *Nihuan Bei* refers to the chanting of the *Nirvana Sutra* or related texts in Buddhist liturgy.
  • 吴天纪四年 - This refers to the fourth year of the *Tianji* era of the Wu kingdom (279 CE), the last kingdom of the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 晋武太康元年 - This is the first year of the *Taikang* era of Emperor Wu of Jin (280 CE), marking the beginning of the Jin dynasty after the fall of Wu.
  • 晋成帝咸和中 - This refers to the *Xianghe* period (326–334 CE) of Emperor Cheng of Jin, during which the rebellion of Su Jun occurred.
  • 苏峻作乱 - Refers to the rebellion of Su Jun, a general who rebelled against the Jin dynasty in 327–329 CE.
  • 司空何充 - He Chong was a high-ranking official of the Jin dynasty who oversaw the rebuilding of the tower after its destruction.
  • 平西将军赵诱 - Zhao You was a general who did not respect the Dharma and was skeptical of the miraculous light from the tower.
  • 三寳 - The 'Three Jewels' of Buddhism: the Buddha, the Dharma, and the Sangha.
  • 道人 - Refers to Buddhist monks or practitioners.
  • 康防 - Likely a name, possibly a Buddhist figure or patron who contributed to the tower's construction or the spread of the image.

Proper Nouns

  • 咸和 - Reign title of Emperor Cheng of Jin.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 害为性若适性为得者 - Ambiguous phrase that can be interpreted as 'If one's nature is harmful, then adapting to it is the right way.' or 'If one's nature is harmful, then adjusting to it is the proper course.'
  • 彼亦逍遥矣 - 'He/she also achieves freedom (or peace of mind).' The term '逍遥' often refers to a state of carefree or untroubled existence.
  • 为是退而注逍遥篇 - 'For this reason, he retreated and wrote the *Xiaoyao* chapter.' The phrase suggests that the author withdrew from public life to compose this work.
  • 羣儒旧学莫不叹伏 - 'All the scholars of the old school were amazed and admired.' The term '羣儒' refers to a group of learned men, and '叹伏' indicates admiration and awe.
  • 后还吴住支硎山寺 - 'Later, he returned to Wu and lived in the Zhi Xing Mountain Temple.' The phrase describes the author's retreat to a specific location.
  • 晚欲入剡 - 'Later, he wanted to enter Shan (a place).' '剡' refers to a region in present-day Zhejiang Province.
  • 谢安在吴与遁书曰 - 'Xie An, who was in Wu, wrote to Duan saying...' Xie An was a prominent politician and military general during the Eastern Jin Dynasty, and '遁' likely refers to the author or another figure.
  • 思君日积计辰倾迟 - 'I think of you day by day, counting the days and feeling impatient.' This phrase expresses Xie An's longing for the author.
  • 知欲还剡自治甚以怅然 - 'Knowing that you want to return to Shan and manage your own affairs, I feel very sad.' The phrase conveys Xie An's emotional response to the author's decision.
  • 人生如寄耳 - 'Life is like a temporary stay.' This is a common philosophical reflection on the transient nature of life.
  • 顷风流得意之事殆为都尽 - 'Recently, the enjoyable and successful events have almost all come to an end.' The phrase suggests a sense of loss or nostalgia.
  • 终日戚戚触事惆怅 - 'I am troubled all day long and feel melancholy when encountering things.' This describes a state of continuous sadness and worry.
  • 唯迟君来以晤言消之一日当千载耳 - 'I only wish for you to come so we can talk and dispel my worries; a day together is worth a thousand years.' This expresses the value of companionship and conversation.
  • 事不异剡而医药不同 - 'The situation is not different from Shan, but the medical treatment is different.' This suggests that the conditions are similar, but the methods of treatment vary.
  • 王羲之时在防稽素闻遁名未之信谓人曰一狂僧耳何足言 - 'Wang Xizhi, who was in Fuzhou, had heard of Duan's reputation but did not believe it, saying to others, 'He is just a mad monk; what is there to talk about?'' This shows Wang Xizhi's initial skepticism.
  • 既至王谓遁曰逍遥篇可闻乎 - 'After arriving, Wang asked Duan, 'Can you tell me about the *Xiaoyao* chapter?'' This indicates Wang Xizhi's interest in the author's work.
  • 遁乃作数千言标揭新理才藻警绝 - 'Duan then wrote several thousand words, introducing new ideas with brilliant and striking language.' This describes the author's literary output.
  • 王遂披襟解带留连 - 'Wang then unfastened his clothes and stayed, engrossed in the conversation.' This shows Wang Xizhi's engagement with the author's ideas.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 愿见瑞相乃梦见道人头白眉长语安云君所注经殊合道理 - Narrative sequence describing a vision or dream. '愿见瑞相' means 'wishing to see auspicious signs,' and '乃梦见道人头白眉长' describes the appearance of a monk in the dream. '语安云君所注经殊合道理' means 'the monk tells An that the scriptures he annotated are very in line with the principles of reason.'
  • 我不得入泥洹住在西域当相助通可时时设食 - Statement made by the monk in the dream. '我不得入泥洹' means 'I cannot enter Nirvana,' '住在西域' means 'I live in the Western Regions,' '当相助通' means 'I will help to communicate,' and '可时时设食' means 'you can set up food from time to time.'
  • 后日诵律至逺公乃知和尚所梦賔头卢也 - Describes a later event. '后日诵律至逺公' means 'later, when reciting the Vinaya rules, An reached the stage of the far monk (Yuan Gong),' '乃知和尚所梦賔头卢也' means 'then he realized that the monk in his dream was Venerable Bhaddasala.'
  • 后至秦建元二十一年正月二十七日忽有异僧形甚庸陋来寺寄宿寺房既窄处之讲堂时维那直殿夜见此僧从窻而出入遽以白安安惊起礼讯问其来意答云特相为来安曰自惟罪深讵可度脱答云甚可以度耳安请问来生所生之处彼乃以手虚拨天之西北即见云开备覩兜率妙胜之报安至其年二月八日忽告众曰吾当去矣是日斋毕无疾而卒塟城内五级寺中是嵗晋太元元年也 - Detailed narrative describing events leading up to An's death. Key phrases include: '后至秦建元二十一年正月二十七日' - 'Later, on the 27th day of the first month of the 21st year of the Jianyuan era of the Former Qin dynasty.' '忽有异僧形甚庸陋来寺寄宿' - 'Suddenly, there appeared a strange monk with an extremely ordinary appearance who came to the temple to stay overnight.' '寺房既窄处之讲堂' - 'Since the temple rooms were narrow, he was placed in the lecture hall.' '时维那直殿夜见此僧从窻而出入遽以白安' - 'At that time, the attendant in charge of the hall saw the monk entering and exiting through the window at night and immediately reported it to An.' '安惊起礼讯问其来意' - 'An woke up in shock, bowed, and asked about his purpose.' '答云特相为来' - 'The monk replied, 'I have come specifically to help you.' '安曰自惟罪深讵可度脱' - 'An said, 'Considering my deep sins, how can I be saved?' '答云甚可以度耳' - 'The monk replied, 'It is very much possible to save you.' '安请问来生所生之处' - 'An asked where he would be reborn.' '彼乃以手虚拨天之西北即见云开备覩兜率妙胜之报' - 'The monk then pointed with his hand to the northwest, and the clouds parted, revealing the magnificent and wonderful reward of the Tushita Heaven.' '安至其年二月八日忽告众曰吾当去矣' - 'On the 8th day of the second month of that year, An suddenly told the congregation, 'I am about to depart.' '是日斋毕无疾而卒塟城内五级寺中' - 'On that day, after the meal, he died peacefully and was buried in the Five-Story Temple within the city.' '是嵗晋太元元年也' - 'That year was the first year of the Taizong era of the Eastern Jin dynasty.'

  • 纂以为美瑞 - Literal meaning: 'Zuan considered it a good omen'; indicates a positive interpretation.
  • 号大殿为龙翔殿 - Literal meaning: 'he called the great hall the Dragon Soaring Hall'; indicates a symbolic renaming.
  • 俄而有黑龙升于当阳九宫门 - Literal meaning: 'soon after, a black dragon rose at the Dangyang Nine Palace Gate'; indicates another supernatural event.
  • 纂改为龙兴门 - Literal meaning: 'Zuan changed it to the Dragon Ascending Gate'; indicates another symbolic renaming.
  • 什奏曰比日龙出逰豕妖来异 - Literal meaning: 'Shi reported, 'Recently, dragons have appeared and pigs have shown strange signs''; indicates a report of supernatural events.
  • 龙者隂出入有时而今屡见则为灾必有下人谋上之变 - Literal meaning: 'Dragons, as yin creatures, appear and disappear at times; now they appear frequently, indicating a disaster and a plot by subordinates against superiors'; indicates a political interpretation.
  • 宜克己修徳以答天戒 - Literal meaning: 'one should cultivate oneself and improve virtue to respond to the heavenly warning'; indicates a moral and political response.
  • 纂不纳 - Literal meaning: 'Zuan did not accept'; indicates a refusal to heed advice.
  • 与什博戏杀棊曰斫胡奴头 - Literal meaning: 'he played a game with Shi and killed the chess piece, saying 'chop off the head of the barbarian slave''; indicates a violent or symbolic act.
  • 不能斫胡奴头胡奴将斫人头 - Literal meaning: 'cannot chop off the head of the barbarian slave, the barbarian slave will chop off human heads'; indicates a prophecy or warning.
  • 此言有防而纂终不悟也 - Literal meaning: 'this statement had a warning, but Zuan never understood'; indicates a missed opportunity.
  • 光弟保有子名超超小字胡奴 - Literal meaning: 'Guang's younger brother Bao had a son named Chao, whose nickname was Hu'nu'; indicates a personal detail.
  • 后果杀纂斩首立其兄隆为主 - Literal meaning: 'later, he killed Zuan, beheaded him, and established his elder brother Long as the ruler'; indicates a political outcome.
  • 时人方验什之言也 - Literal meaning: 'at the time, people verified Shi's words'; indicates the fulfillment of a prophecy.
  • 什住凉积年吕光父 - Literal meaning: 'Shi lived in Liang for many years, and was the father of Lü Guang'; indicates a historical detail.

[1096]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 苻坚已亡竟不相见 - Means 'after Fu Jian died, they never met'; '竟' means ultimately or finally, and '相见' means to meet.
  • 姚苌僭有闗中 - Means 'Yao Chang usurped control of Guanzhong'; '僭' means to usurp or claim illegitimately, and '闗中' refers to the Guanzhong region.
  • 姚谋不许东入 - Means 'Yao did not allow him to enter the east'; '姚谋' means Yao's plan, and '不许' means did not allow.
  • 及苌卒子兴袭位 - Means 'when Yao died, his son Xing succeeded him'; '袭位' means to succeed to a position.
  • 复遣敦请 - Means 'he sent again to invite'; '复' means again, and '敦请' means to earnestly invite.
  • 逍遥园葱变为茝 - Means 'in the Xiyao Garden, scallions turned into orchids'; '变为' means turned into, and '茝' means orchids.
  • 以为美瑞 - Means 'considered it a good omen'; '美瑞' means good omen.
  • 谓智人应入 - Means 'thought that a wise person should come'; '谓' means to think, and '应入' means should come.
  • 至五月兴遣陇西公硕徳西伐吕隆 - Means 'In May, Xing sent Longxi Prince Shuode to the west to attack Lü Long'; '遣' means to send, and '西伐' means to attack to the west.
  • 隆军大破 - Means 'Lü's army was heavily defeated'; '大破' means heavily defeated.
  • 至九月隆上表归降 - Means 'In September, Lü submitted a petition to surrender'; '上表' means to submit a petition, and '归降' means to surrender.
  • 方得迎什入闗 - Means 'they finally managed to invite Sheshi into the region'; '方得' means finally managed, and '迎什入闗' means to invite Sheshi into the region.
  • 以其年十二月二十日至于长安 - Means 'on the 20th day of the 12th month of that year, he arrived in Chang'an'; '以其年' means of that year, and '至于' means arrived at.
  • 兴待以国师之礼 - Means 'Xing treated him with the respect due to a national teacher'; '待以' means treated with, and '国师' means national teacher.
  • 甚见优宠 - Means 'he was greatly favored'; '甚' means greatly, and '见优宠' means was favored.
  • 自大法东被始于汉明 - Means 'since the great teachings were brought eastward, starting from Emperor Ming of Han'; '大法' means great teachings, '东被' means brought eastward, and '始于' means started from.
  • 经论渐多 - Means 'the number of sutras and commentaries gradually increased'; '经论' means sutras and commentaries, and '渐多' means gradually increased.
  • 而支竺所出多滞文格义 - Means 'but the translations by Zhi and Zhu were often difficult to understand'; '滞文' means difficult to understand, and '格义' means interpretation.
  • 锐志讲集 - Means 'he had a keen interest in lectures and gatherings'; '锐志' means keen interest, and '讲集' means lectures and gatherings.
  • 什既至止仍请入西明閤 - Means 'after Sheshi arrived, he was invited to enter the Ximing Hall'; '仍请' means still invited, and '西明閤' means Ximing Hall.
  • 及逍遥园译出众经 - Means 'and in the Xiyao Garden, he translated many sutras'; '译出' means translated, and '众经' means many sutras.
  • 什既率多谙诵 - Means 'Sheshi was well-versed in many sutras'; '谙诵' means well-versed.
  • 无不究尽 - Means 'he thoroughly understood all of them'; '究尽' means thoroughly understood.
  • 转能汉言 - Means 'he became proficient in Chinese'; '转能' means became proficient, and '汉言' means Chinese.
  • 音译流便 - Means 'his translations were smooth and fluent'; '流便' means smooth and fluent.
  • 既览旧经义多纰僻 - Means 'after reviewing the old sutras, he found many errors'; '纰僻' means errors.
  • 皆由先度失音 - Means 'all due to previous mistranslations of sounds'; '先度' means previous, and '失音' means mistranslations of sounds.
  • 不与梵本相应 - Means 'which did not correspond to the original Sanskrit texts'; '相应' means correspond.
  • 于是兴使沙 - Means 'so Xing sent Sheshi'; '使' means to send, and '沙' likely refers to Sheshi (the translator).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 苻坚 - Refers to Fu Jian, the ruler of Former Qin during the Sixteen Kingdoms period.
  • 姚苌 - Refers to Yao Chang, the founder of Later Qin.
  • 吕隆 - Refers to Lü Long, a ruler of the Western Liang state.
  • 长安 - Refers to Chang'an, the capital of Later Qin.
  • 支竺 - Refers to the translators Zhi Qian and Zhu Falan, who were early Buddhist translators.
  • 西明閤 - Refers to the Ximing Hall, a place in Chang'an where Buddhist texts were translated.
  • 逍遥园 - Refers to the Xiyao Garden, another place in Chang'an where Buddhist texts were translated.

[1097]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 門僧防僧遷法欽道流道恒道標僧叡僧肇等八百余人谘受什 - A list of monks who sought teachings from Kumarajiva, representing a collective of spiritual seekers.
  • 防更令出大品 - Kumarajiva's disciple was instructed to produce a new version of the 'Great Treatise'.
  • 什持梵本興執舊經以相讐校 - Kumarajiva held the Sanskrit text, while Sengzhao held the old Chinese translation. They compared the two versions.
  • 其新文異舊者義皆圓通 - The new text, where it differed from the old, was found to be more coherent and logically sound.
  • 眾心惬伏莫不欣赞 - The audience was pleased and approved of the new translation.
  • 什为人神情鉴彻傲岸出羣 - Kumarajiva's personality is described as perceptive, clear-minded, and superior to others.
  • 应机领会鲜有其匹 - Kumarajiva's ability to respond to situations and grasp their essence was rare and unparalleled.
  • 且笃性仁厚泛爱为心 - Kumarajiva's character is described as kind, benevolent, and loving.
  • 虚已善诱终日无倦 - Kumarajiva was humble and skilled in guiding others, and he never tired of teaching.
  • 姚兴常谓什曰大师聪明超悟天下莫二 - Yao Xing praised Kumarajiva's intelligence and insight, which he considered unmatched in the world.
  • 若一旦后世何可使法种无嗣 - A rhetorical question expressing Yao Xing's concern that if Kumarajiva were to pass away, the Dharma would have no successor.
  • 遂以妓女十人逼令受之 - Yao Xing forced Kumarajiva to take ten courtesans, likely to ensure his lineage.
  • 自尔已来不住僧坊别立舍供给丰盈 - After this event, Kumarajiva no longer stayed in the monastery but had a separate residence with ample provisions.
  • 每至讲説常先自説譬譬如臭泥中生莲华但采莲华勿取臭泥也 - A metaphor where Kumarajiva compares the Dharma to a lotus growing in dirty mud, advising to pick the lotus but not the mud, meaning to accept the teachings but not the worldly attachments.
  • 什初在兹从卑摩罗叉律师受律卑摩后入闗中什闻至欣然师敬尽礼 - Kumarajiva's early life in Kucha, where he studied monastic rules under the monk Bimolochaka, who later went to the Western Regions. Kumarajiva was pleased and showed great respect when he heard of Bimolochaka's arrival.
  • 卑摩未知被逼之事因问什曰汝于汉地大有重縁受法弟子可有防人 - Bimolochaka, unaware of the forced situation, asked Kumarajiva if he had many disciples in China.
  • 什答云汉境经律未备新经及诸论等 - Kumarajiva replied that the Chinese translation of the scriptures and monastic rules was incomplete, and there were new scriptures and treatises that needed translation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 多是什所传出 - Indicates that most of the teachings were transmitted by Kumarajiva.
  • 三千徒众皆从什受法 - Means that three thousand disciples all received the Dharma from Kumarajiva.
  • 但什累业障深故不受师敬耳 - Suggests that although Kumarajiva had deep karmic obstacles, he did not receive the respect due to a teacher.
  • 吾与此子戏别三百余年杳然未期迟有遇于来生耳 - Means that the monk believed he had met Kumarajiva in a previous life and hoped to meet again in the future life.
  • 未及致力转觉危殆于是力疾与众僧告别曰 - Indicates that Kumarajiva felt his condition worsening and bid farewell to the monks.
  • 因法相遇殊未尽心方复后世恻怆何言 - Expresses Kumarajiva's regret that they did not fully benefit from their encounter in this life.
  • 自以暗昧谬充传谭 - Kumarajiva humbly acknowledges his own limitations in transmitting the teachings.
  • 凡所出经论三百余卷唯日诵一部未及删繁存其本防必无差失 - Kumarajiva's commitment to ensuring the accuracy of his translations.
  • 今于众前发诚实誓若所传无谬者当使焚身之后舌不焦烂 - Kumarajiva's vow that his tongue would not burn if his translations were accurate.
  • 即于逍遥园依外国法以火焚尸薪灭形碎唯舌不灰耳 - Describes the cremation of Kumarajiva's body according to foreign customs, with his tongue remaining unburned.

[1100]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 病狀頓除 - The illness symptoms suddenly disappeared. Common expression in classical Chinese to denote sudden recovery or miraculous healing.
  • 得道之士 - Someone who has attained spiritual enlightenment or the Dao.
  • 試人 - To test people. Common phrase in Daoist and Buddhist texts to describe divine or enlightened beings testing mortals.
  • 作礼悔过 - To perform rites of repentance. A Buddhist term for making amends or expressing remorse.
  • 诸君诚契并至同当入道 - You all have sincere aspirations; together you shall enter the Dao. A formal encouragement to those who are spiritually ready.
  • 宿学业浅 - His learning is shallow. A common phrase to describe someone who lacks depth in their studies.
  • 此世未得 - He has not attained this life. May be a reference to not achieving enlightenment in this lifetime.
  • 植根深 - If the root is deep. Refers to deep spiritual foundation.
  • 现世得愿 - Attain one's wishes in this life. A common Buddhist expression.
  • 因而留之 - Therefore, he kept him. A concise way of expressing a decision based on a prior conclusion.
  • 道俗宗之 - Both the monastic and lay communities revered him. A standard way to describe someone's influence across society.
  • 出防祥记 - From *Fang Xiang Ji* (Record of Divine Signs). A reference to a classical source, likely a Buddhist or Daoist text.
  • 李恒沙门 - Monk Li Heng. A name of a historical figure, likely a Buddhist monk.
  • 福报将至而复祸来随之 - Blessings are about to come, but misfortune will follow. A common saying in Buddhist and Daoist texts about the duality of fate.
  • 守贫脩道不仕宦者福増祸灭 - If one guards against poverty, cultivates the Dao, and does not pursue officialdom, blessings increase and misfortune decreases. A moral statement about the benefits of monastic life.
  • 性躁 - Impatient nature. A common characterization of a person’s disposition.
  • 寒门 - Poor family. A term used to describe someone from a low social class.
  • 荣途贵贱 - Prosperous path, high or low rank. Refers to social status and career prospects.
  • 带金紫 - To wear golden and purple robes. A metaphor for high official rank in ancient China.
  • 三郡 - Three prefectures. A term referring to administrative divisions.
  • 一郡止者亦为善道 - If one can stop at one prefecture, that is also a good path. A metaphor for contentment or moderation.
  • 富贵何顾后患 - Why worry about future troubles if one is rich and powerful? A statement of short-sightedness or greed.
  • 身满一牀 - His body filled the entire bed. A literal description of a miraculous or supernatural phenomenon.
  • 大鸟跱屋梁上 - A great bird perched on the eaves. A metaphor for a divine or supernatural being.
  • 天晓而形如旧 - At dawn, he returned to his original form. A common narrative in classical Chinese texts describing a deity or spirit revealing themselves and then disappearing.
  • 神人 - Divine person. A term used for a supernatural or enlightened being.
  • 不能精至 - Could not be fully devoted. A description of someone who is not fully committed to a path.
  • 西阳江夏庐江太守 - Governor of Xiyang, Jiangxia, and Luyang. These are ancient Chinese administrative regions.
  • 龙骧将军 - General of the Dragon’s Might. A military title.
  • 太兴中 - During the Taixing period. This refers to a reign period of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 预钱凤之乱 - Involved in the rebellion of Qian Feng. Qian Feng was a historical figure in the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 被诛 - Was executed. A common term for capital punishment.
  • 出法苑珠林 - From *Fayuan Zhulín* (Jewels of the Dharma Grove). A reference to a classical Buddhist text, likely a collection of stories or anecdotes.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 光悦 - Likely a name of a monk or layperson who was miraculously healed.
  • 华馨朗 - Names of individuals who witnessed the miracle and recognized the sage.
  • 得道之士 - A term from Daoist and Buddhist texts referring to one who has attained spiritual enlightenment.
  • 比丘 - A term for a Buddhist monk.
  • 惠可 - A historical figure, the second patriarch of Chan (Zen) Buddhism. This suggests a connection to early Chan tradition.
  • 法朗 - A monk who initially doubted the spiritual value of reading sutras, indicating a common critique of monastic life in early Buddhism.
  • 李恒 - A historical figure, likely a layperson who later became a government official.
  • 沙门 - A term for Buddhist monks.
  • 读经 - Reading sutras. A central practice in Buddhist monastic life.
  • 慈爱心 - Compassionate heart. A key Buddhist virtue.
  • 礼觐 - Rites of homage. A formal act of respect and veneration.
  • 入道 - To enter the Dao. A term used in both Daoism and Buddhism to refer to spiritual awakening.
  • 宿学业浅 - Superficial learning. A common criticism in classical Chinese texts of those who lack depth in study.
  • 现世得愿 - Attain wishes in this life. A common Buddhist concept.
  • 道俗宗之 - Revered by both monastic and lay communities. Indicates widespread respect and influence.
  • 防祥记 - *Fang Xiang Ji* (Record of Divine Signs). Likely a collection of miraculous stories or religious anecdotes.
  • 法苑珠林 - *Fayuan Zhulín* (Jewels of the Dharma Grove). A well-known Buddhist text from the Tang dynasty, compiling various religious stories and teachings.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太平廣記 - A classical Chinese collection of supernatural, historical, and anecdotal stories, compiled in the 10th century during the Song Dynasty; one of the most important sources for Chinese folklore and religious tales.

  • 釋寳志 - A historical Buddhist monk from the Liang Dynasty (502–557), known for his eccentric behavior, prophecies, and association with the imperial court; often depicted in Chinese art and literature as a mystical figure.
  • 杯渡 - A legendary monk known for his ability to travel by floating on wooden cups, hence his name; a common figure in Chinese Buddhist folklore.

  • 李家 - The Li family, a common surname in Chinese history, though no specific historical figure is named here.

  • 邑吏访之果得焉及君之来又依然长沙之貌 - This sentence is somewhat ambiguous. It could be interpreted as 'The local officials visited and indeed found him, and when you arrived, you still looked like the appearance of Changsha.'
  • 然自梦及今十一年矣故讶君之晚也 - This sentence introduces a contrast or continuation. It could be interpreted as 'However, from the dream to now, it has been eleven years, so I was surprised at your late arrival.'
  • 已而悲惋泣下数行 - This sentence describes an emotional reaction. It could be interpreted as 'Then I was filled with sorrow and wept several times.'
  • 因出经笥示之广愿执履锡为门弟子 - This sentence describes a sequence of actions. It could be interpreted as 'So he took out the bamboo box containing the scriptures and showed it to Guang, who wished to serve as his disciple and receive his teachings.'
  • 君且去翌日当再来广受教而还 - This sentence describes a sequence of actions. It could be interpreted as 'You should leave now and come back tomorrow, and Guang received the teachings and returned.'
  • 明日至居而照已遁去 - This sentence describes a sequence of events. It could be interpreted as 'The next day, when he arrived at his residence, Zha had already disappeared.'
  • 莫知其所适时 - This sentence describes a state of uncertainty. It could be interpreted as 'No one knows where he went or what time it was.'
  • 梁普通七年生 - This sentence describes a specific year. It could be interpreted as 'Born in the seventh year of the Tongping era of the Liang dynasty.'
  • 按梁史普通七年岁在丙午 - This sentence describes a specific year. It could be interpreted as 'According to the Liang dynasty's history, the seventh year of Tongping was the year of Bingwu.'
  • 至唐元和十年乙未 - This sentence describes a specific year. It could be interpreted as 'To the tenth year of the Yuanhe era of the Tang dynasty, which was the year of Yichou.'
  • 则与照言果符矣 - This sentence describes a conclusion. It could be interpreted as 'So the words of Zha were indeed correct.'
  • 愚常以梁陈二史校其所说 - This sentence describes a method of verification. It could be interpreted as 'I often compare his words with the histories of the Liang and Chen dynasties.'
  • 颇有同者 - This sentence describes a similarity. It could be interpreted as 'There are many similarities.'
  • 由是益信其不诬矣 - This sentence describes a conclusion. It could be interpreted as 'Therefore, I have become more convinced that he was not lying.'
  • 出宣室志 - This sentence describes the source of the text. It could be interpreted as 'From the *Xuan Shi Zhi*.'
